title: Key compliance attributes of internal audit - Canada.ca
content:
## Key compliance attributes of internal audit
This information is being reported in compliance with section A.2.2.3 of the Treasury Board Directive on Internal Audit . For additional information, please see the Treasury Board Secretariat of Canada guidance .

## Results for Key Compliance Attributes of Internal Audit as of September 22, 2025
Performance indicators
Key compliance attributes
Results as of September 22, 2025
Do internal auditors in departments have the training required to do the job effectively? Are multidisciplinary teams in place to address diverse risks?
Do internal auditors in departments have the training required to do the job effectively?
Are multidisciplinary teams in place to address diverse risks?
Percentage of staff with an internal audit or accounting designation (Certified Internal Auditor (CIA), Chartered Professional Accountant (CPA))
Percentage of staff with an internal audit or accounting designation (CIA, CPA) in progress
Percentage of staff holding other designations (CGAP, CISA, etc.)
Is internal audit work performed in conformance with the international standards for the profession of internal audit as required by Treasury Board policy?
Yes, the internal audit work is performed in conformance with the International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing, as required by Treasury Board policy. This is confirmed through the annual Internal Self-assessment Review of the Quality Assurance and Improvement Program, which evaluates the performance of the internal audit function against the standards set by the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA). The review assesses: Adherence to the IIA’s Code of Ethics and Standards. Alignment with Treasury Board’s Policy on Internal Audit. Effectiveness of audit planning, execution, and reporting. Continuous improvement initiatives within the audit function. This self-assessment is documented and reported annually as part of the DAC Annual Plan to ensure transparency and accountability in the audit process.
Yes, the internal audit work is performed in conformance with the International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing, as required by Treasury Board policy. This is confirmed through the annual Internal Self-assessment Review of the Quality Assurance and Improvement Program, which evaluates the performance of the internal audit function against the standards set by the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA).
The review assesses:
- Adherence to the IIA’s Code of Ethics and Standards.
- Alignment with Treasury Board’s Policy on Internal Audit.
- Effectiveness of audit planning, execution, and reporting.
- Continuous improvement initiatives within the audit function.
This self-assessment is documented and reported annually as part of the DAC Annual Plan to ensure transparency and accountability in the audit process.
DAC Fiscal Year 2025-2026 Work Plan
Five-Year External Assessment of the Internal Audit Function The next five-year External Assessment of the Internal Audit Function is planned to be presented to the DAC at its February 2026 meeting. The previous external assessment was completed in 2021.
Five-Year External Assessment of the Internal Audit Function
The next five-year External Assessment of the Internal Audit Function is planned to be presented to the DAC at its February 2026 meeting. The previous external assessment was completed in 2021.
Are the risk-based audit plans submitted to audit committees and approved by deputy heads implemented as planned with resulting reports published? Is management acting on audit recommendations for improvements to departmental processes?
Are the risk-based audit plans submitted to audit committees and approved by deputy heads implemented as planned with resulting reports published?
Is management acting on audit recommendations for improvements to departmental processes?
Risk-Based Audit Plans (RBAPs): The approved RBAPs are being implemented as planned, and the resulting audit reports are published in accordance with Treasury Board Policy on Internal Audit. Management continues to act on audit recommendations to strengthen departmental processes, with progress monitored and reported to the DAC semi-annually.
Risk-Based Audit Plans (RBAPs): The approved RBAPs are being implemented as planned, and the resulting audit reports are published in accordance with Treasury Board Policy on Internal Audit.
Management continues to act on audit recommendations to strengthen departmental processes, with progress monitored and reported to the DAC semi-annually.
Refer to: "Risk-based audit plan (RBAP) and related information"
Is internal audit credible and adding value in support of the mandate and strategic objectives of the organization?
Average overall usefulness rating of the internal audit function based on surveys of senior management (ADM-level or equivalent) responsible for areas audited was 83% in 2024-2025.
